user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Migrate the TC387 GTM TOM 3‑phase inverter PWM to TC4D7 using eGTM ATOM and the IfxEgtm_Pwm unified API.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate six complementary, center‑aligned PWM outputs at 20 kHz with ~1 µs dead‑time (rising=1e-6, falling=1e-6) and initial duties U=25%, V=50%, W=75%, with synchronous start and synchronous duty updates; HS active high and LS active low; push‑pull outputs with cmosAutomotiveSpeed1 pad driver.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use the unified high-level IfxEgtm_Pwm driver on TC4D7 EGTM with ATOM contiguous channels for 3 complementary pairs and on-channel DTM. Configure center-aligned base channel and 5 sync channels with syncStart/syncUpdate enabled in the driver config. Use EGTM CMU GCLK=100 MHz (10 ns) to achieve 20 kHz period (5000 ticks) and ~1 µs dead-time (100 ticks). Route pins via IfxEgtm_PinMap macros to the preferred P02.x TOUT pairs on KIT_A3G_TC4D7_LITE (validated defaults point to ATOM0 CH0–5). Initial duties [0.25, 0.5, 0.75] are applied at init; runtime duty changes use synchronous update.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified high-level PWM driver supports center alignment, complementary outputs with DTM dead-time, synchronous start/update, and batch channel updates. It directly matches the migration mapping IfxGtm_Pwm → IfxEgtm_Pwm and is available in the template.

runtime_function.name: updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Synchronous duty updates for U, V, W phases (complementary pairs) latched on the PWM period event.

driver_changes: IfxScuWdt.h → IfxWtu.h (watchdog family migration; use available IfxWtu APIs in the template)
migration_notes.context_preserved: The migration keeps the original 3‑phase inverter behavior on the new TC4D7 target: six complementary, center‑aligned PWM outputs at 20 kHz, ~1 µs dead‑time, HS/LS polarity as specified, synchronous start and update, and push‑pull with cmosAutomotiveSpeed1. The unified IfxEgtm_Pwm API encapsulates ATOM channel setup, DTM dead‑time, pin routing via IfxEgtm_PinMap macros, and synchronous control to meet the user’s timing and polarity requirements. Channel allocation uses contiguous ATOM channels (ATOM0 CH0–5 on KIT_A3G_TC4D7_LITE P02.x TOUTs) per the provided hardware validation.

PWM.rationale: Driver locked by refiner node (user explicit or engine-scored)
